title: carthamus tenuis subsp. foliosus
intro: Carthamus tenuis subsp. foliosus, also known as leafy safflower, is a subspecies of the safflower plant (Carthamus tenuis) native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Leafy safflower has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and antimicrobial properties. Leafy safflower is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used topically in the form of creams and ointments to soothe skin irritations.
